summary: Matched and linked Samus missile item translation unit by switching itsamusmissile.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, reorganizing local function order, tightening item-specific prototypes, and applying small control-flow/type cleanups needed for matching. The PR also adjusted itlinkbomb.c with equivalent boolean-style conditionals and an inline-helper replacement, likely to preserve matching after shared item/header changes. No review discussion was present; PR body only noted remaining item TUs/functions.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...), linking the TU as matching.

Moved the Samus missile ItemStateTable definition it_803F7340 near the top of itsamusmissile.c, with states 0 and 1 using Samus missile anim/phys/coll callbacks and states 2 and 3 using itSamusmissile_UnkMotion3_Anim.

Reordered small Samus missile callbacks in itsamusmissile.c: collision callbacks and itSamusmissile_UnkMotion3_Anim were moved closer to their related motion code instead of being defined before the factory/lifetime functions.
Changed itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Anim and itSamusmissile_UnkMotion1_Anim return types from s32/long-style declarations to bool while still returning false.
Updated itsamusmissile.h prototypes to prefer Item_GObj* over HSD_GObj* for several item-local functions, including it_802B63F8, it_802B66A8, it_802B6A60, and the motion anim callbacks.
Simplified it_802B64FC control flow by replacing goto-to-return blocks with direct early returns while preserving vector/angle logic.
Adjusted includes in itsamusmissile.c: removed unused baselib/jobj.h, math.h, and it/it_266F.h from the shown excerpt; added it/itgroundcoll.h; kept baselib/mtx.h via angle-bracket include.
In itlinkbomb.c, changed repeated `it_80272C6C(gobj) == 0` checks to `!it_80272C6C(gobj)`.

In itlinkbomb.c, removed a static inline helper for motion 3 and replaced its call site with existing function it_8029EC34(gobj); also changed inline2 to call fn_8029E21C(gobj) instead of it_8029D9A4(gobj, 1, 0) when the guard is false.
smart_moves: Promoted the TU to Matching only after aligning function signatures, state-table placement, and local control flow, rather than only toggling configure.py.
Used bool callback return types for item animation/collision functions that semantically return false, matching common ItemStateTable callback style.
Reduced goto noise in it_802B64FC with early returns, a common decomp cleanup that can preserve generated code shape when branch structure permits it.
Reused existing it_8029EC34 in itlinkbomb.c instead of maintaining a duplicated static inline helper, reducing local inline complexity.
Kept item APIs item-specific by using Item_GObj* in the header for functions that operate on item objects, while GET_ITEM still handles the underlying object access in implementation.
decomp_lessons: For item TUs, matching may require the global ItemStateTable to appear in the correct source order relative to functions, not merely to contain the right callback entries.
When an item callback always returns false, declaring it as bool can better match the state-table callback type and clean up header consistency.
Replacing `func(...) == 0` with `!func(...)` can be a matching-relevant source expression change in this codebase, especially around item animation guards.
Existing helper functions can sometimes replace static inline clones and produce a better or equivalent match with less code.
Header prototype cleanup is part of matching work: mismatched HSD_GObj*/Item_GObj* declarations and long/s32/bool callback types can obstruct clean linking or future reuse.
Evidence for why each exact reorder was required is limited because there were no review comments and the PR body was minimal.
naming_conventions: Samus missile item symbols use the mixed-case item prefix `itSamusmissile_` for motion callbacks and address-style names such as `it_802B63F8` for unnamed functions.
Motion callback names retain `UnkMotionN_Anim`, `UnkMotionN_Phys`, and `UnkMotionN_Coll` until better semantic names are known.
The Samus missile state table keeps an address-style global name, `it_803F7340`, rather than inventing a descriptive name.
Item-specific attributes are named `itSamusMissileAttributes* attrs` or `sa` in the implementation.
Item object parameters in the header are preferably typed as `Item_GObj* gobj` for item functions; generic `HSD_GObj*` is avoided where item-specific typing is known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Switch the build configuration from NonMatching to Matching only after source-level shape changes are in place.
Move small callback definitions to positions that satisfy the compiler/linker ordering needed for a matching TU.
Use direct early returns instead of labels/gotos when they produce the same branch structure and cleaner C.
Use `!predicate` instead of `predicate == 0` in item guard conditions where that expression form matches expected output.
Add or remove includes to match actual dependencies and avoid placeholder or obsolete declarations leaking into a matched TU.
Preserve PAD_STACK use where still needed, as seen in nearby item functions such as itlinkbomb and it_802B70A0 in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename Samus missile motion callbacks from UnkMotion names to semantic names.
Search for other item TUs promoted from NonMatching to Matching by changing configure.py Object entries.
Look for matching PRs that move ItemStateTable definitions to resolve ordering or linking issues.
Search for follow-up changes to itlinkbomb.c around it_8029EC34, fn_8029E21C, or motion 3 animation behavior.
Check whether other item headers were converted from HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj* for item-local function prototypes.
